behavior_summary: U8P_MUSHROOM::look is a minimal U8P_MUSHROOM::look/examine handler for the mushroom object. When the player inspects the mushroom the script emits a single bark text "mushroom" and then suspends/ends the U8P_MUSHROOM::look process.
There is no branching, menu interaction, global flag read/write, or external function calls; the routine simply presents a short description and terminates.
notable_control_flow: Entry: invoked as the object's U8P_MUSHROOM::look/examine process (process type 0x0208).
Action: perform a single Bark/DialogueLine output with the text "mushroom".
Termination: suspend/terminate the U8P_MUSHROOM::look process immediately after the bark; no further steps or conditions.
open_questions: Is the displayed bark text localized or meant to include the trailing spaces shown in the raw sample ("mushroom  ")?
Is this object ever extended with additional behaviors (use, pick up) elsewhere or is it purely descriptive?

main_function.name: U8P_MUSHROOM::look
